title: The Inventory Reservation Retry
alert: Repeated reservation requests are exhausting available stock.

briefing.objective: Contain duplicate inventory reservations without changing the availability contract for distinct orders.
briefing.successCriterion: The Python unittest suite must show that a retried order owns one reservation while distinct orders remain independently reserved.
briefing.rootCause: The reservation book appends every request and never checks whether the order already holds a reservation.
briefing.evidence: The acceptance suite compares the reservation ledger after repeated submissions and verifies that other orders are not removed.

telemetry.impact: Available inventory is falling faster than completed orders, causing unnecessary out-of-stock warnings.

events.message: Reservation count diverged from unique checkout orders.
Order ord-102 retried after a gateway timeout.
The ledger recorded two holds for ord-102.

stakeholderMessages.author: Maya - PM
stakeholderMessages.body: Customers are seeing items marked unavailable while checkout is still retrying. Can we stop the extra holds without dropping valid orders?

stakeholderMessages.author: Jon - Senior Eng
stakeholderMessages.body: What property must hold for one order across a retry, and what must remain true for a different order?

stakeholderMessages.author: AI Pair
stakeholderMessages.body: I found three ways to change reservation handling. I would separate duplicate membership from display order before selecting one.
